trivia: The Gulfstream V opened the ultra-long-range era in 1995: nonstop from New York to Tokyo, no fuel stop. The refined G550 followed in 2003 and became the best-selling large-cabin Gulfstream of its generation.

Governments and air forces love this platform: special-mission G550s fly as radar testbeds, signals aircraft, and VIP transports on every continent, so the GLF5 you caught may not be a private jet at all.
funFacts: The G550 won the 2003 Collier Trophy for aviation achievement.
Israel's Nachshon fleet turned G550s into flying radar stations.
